title: Extract shared project_id guideline for reconciliation stage prompts
analysis: The project_id guideline was added to all three stage system prompts in commit 5263576 then removed in commit e2fce78 ('simplify') as 'redundant' since base.py injects a Reconciliation Context block into the payload. However, the base.py injection provides the project_id VALUE but doesn't explicitly instruct the agent to USE it in every call — that's what the system prompt guideline does. The removed lines were copy-pasted with near-identical wording, differing only in the parenthetical tool list. Stage 2's tool list was incomplete (omitted task-mutation tools). The fix: (1) create a shared template constant _PROJECT_ID_GUIDELINE in prompts/__init__.py with a {tools} placeholder, using 'Reconciliation Context block' phrasing matching base.py's section label; (2) re-add the guideline to each stage's system prompt using the shared constant with stage-specific tool lists; (3) ensure Stage 2's tool list includes ALL tools it has access to (memory + task mutation).

steps.description: Write tests for _PROJECT_ID_GUIDELINE shared constant: importable from prompts package, contains {tools} placeholder, contains 'Reconciliation Context block' phrasing, .format(tools=...) produces correct string
Add _PROJECT_ID_GUIDELINE template constant to prompts/__init__.py: '- Always include the `project_id` from the Reconciliation Context block in every fused-memory MCP call ({tools}).'

Write test that Stage 1 system prompt contains the project_id guideline with correct memory-only tools (search, add_memory, delete_memory, get_entity, get_episodes, get_status) and 'Reconciliation Context block' phrasing
Update stage1.py to import _PROJECT_ID_GUIDELINE from prompts package and embed it in STAGE1_SYSTEM_PROMPT with Stage 1's memory tool list via .format()

Write test that Stage 2 system prompt contains the project_id guideline with ALL tools including task-mutation tools (search, add_memory, delete_memory, get_entity, get_episodes, get_status, get_tasks, get_task, set_task_status, add_task, update_task, add_subtask, remove_task, add_dependency, remove_dependency)
Update stage2.py to import _PROJECT_ID_GUIDELINE from prompts package and embed it in STAGE2_SYSTEM_PROMPT with Stage 2's complete tool list (memory + task-mutation tools)
Write test that Stage 3 system prompt contains the project_id guideline with read-only tools (search, get_entity, get_episodes, get_status, get_tasks, get_task) and does NOT contain write tools
Update stage3.py to import _PROJECT_ID_GUIDELINE from prompts package and embed it in STAGE3_SYSTEM_PROMPT with Stage 3's read-only tool list
Write consistency test: all three stage prompts share the same prefix text before the tool list parenthetical, proving they use the same shared constant rather than copy-pasted variants

design_decisions.decision: Use a template string constant (_PROJECT_ID_GUIDELINE) with {tools} placeholder rather than a function
design_decisions.rationale: A template string is simpler and more transparent than a function. Each stage imports it and calls .format(tools=...) at module level, keeping the prompt constants as plain strings. This matches the existing pattern where prompts are simple string constants.

design_decisions.decision: Place the shared constant in prompts/__init__.py rather than a new _shared.py
design_decisions.rationale: prompts/__init__.py already exists (with only a docstring). Using it avoids creating a new file and follows the Python convention of putting package-level shared definitions in __init__.py. The constant is small enough that a separate _shared.py is over-engineering.

design_decisions.decision: Use 'Reconciliation Context block' phrasing instead of 'payload context'
design_decisions.rationale: The base.py injection uses the heading '## Reconciliation Context' (line 116). Referring to it as 'Reconciliation Context block' in the guideline creates an unambiguous cross-reference that the agent can follow to find the actual project_id value.

design_decisions.decision: Stage 2 tool list includes ALL task-mutation tools, not just read tools
design_decisions.rationale: Stage 2 (TaskKnowledgeSync) has STAGE2_DISALLOWED = DISALLOW_BUILTIN only — meaning it has full access to both memory and task tools. The original implementation incorrectly used the same tool list as Stage 1 (memory-only), omitting set_task_status, add_task, update_task, add_subtask, remove_task, add_dependency, remove_dependency.

design_decisions.decision: Convert stage prompt strings to f-strings to embed the formatted guideline
design_decisions.rationale: The cleanest way to embed _PROJECT_ID_GUIDELINE.format(tools=...) into the triple-quoted prompt constants is to make them f-strings. An alternative (string concatenation) would break the visual structure. A module-level _S1_PID = _PROJECT_ID_GUIDELINE.format(...) variable keeps the f-string substitution simple.

design_decisions.decision: Add tests to existing test_stages.py rather than a new test file
design_decisions.rationale: test_stages.py already contains TestStage3PromptAlignment which tests prompt content, plus TestDisallowedToolLists. Adding a new TestProjectIdGuideline class to the same file follows the established pattern of keeping stage-related tests together.

reuse.how: Already exists with a docstring — extend it with the _PROJECT_ID_GUIDELINE constant rather than creating a new file
Follow the TestStage3PromptAlignment pattern for testing prompt content. Reuse the stage_mock_deps fixture and import patterns from TestStageSubclasses.
Cross-reference this authoritative list of task-mutation tools when building Stage 2's tool list, ensuring the guideline's tool list matches the actual permissions
The '## Reconciliation Context' heading is the label the guideline references — no changes needed to base.py, but the guideline's phrasing ('from the Reconciliation Context block') must match
